commands.review_summary:
The PR updates message-provider tool filtering to skip descriptors whose `name` getter throws only when provider allow/deny policy must inspect names, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +31. Total +60 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main reads `tool.name` in `filterToolsByMessageProvider()` before checking whether provider policy applies, and the PR body records a focused red repro for a throwing getter; I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because no blocking finding was identified; this maintainer-owned draft needs normal readiness and CI handling.

target.title: fix(agents): guard message provider tool name reads
target.body:
## Summary
- Guard `filterToolsByMessageProvider()` against tool descriptors whose `name` getter throws while provider allow/deny policy is applied.
- Avoid reading descriptor names when no message-provider policy exists, preserving the original tool list for providers such as `guildchat`.
- Add focused regression coverage for unreadable names in the voice-policy path and the no-policy path.

## Verification
- Red repro before fix: `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts -t "omits unreadable tool names" --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000` failed with `fuzzed unreadable tool name` at `src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts:38`.
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts -t "omits unreadable tool names" --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.84)`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Message-provider tool policy filtering no longer crashes when a malformed tool descriptor has an unreadable `name` getter; provider policy filtering omits that malformed descriptor and keeps healthy allowed siblings.
Real environment tested: Local linked OpenClaw worktree on current `origin/main` branch `fuzz-tool-schema-next75-20260603`, using the repo Vitest wrapper for the focused agent policy test file.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`, plus focused formatter/lint/diff checks listed above.
Evidence after fix: The full focused test file passed with 1 file / 9 tests; direct scoped `oxfmt`, direct scoped `oxlint`, `git diff --check`, and local autoreview passed.
Observed result after fix: The synthetic unreadable-name descriptor is dropped only when a provider policy must inspect names, `tts` is still denied for voice providers, and providers without a policy return the original tool descriptors without reading names.
What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:changed` was not run from this linked worktree. Blacksmith Testbox is unauthenticated locally, available disk is under 1GiB, `pnpm exec oxfmt` tried dependency reconciliation and aborted, and the repo `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` wrapper was blocked before scoped lint by unrelated existing Discord boundary d.ts errors in `extensions/discord/src/voice/receive-recovery.ts`.
target.author: vincentkoc
